name: Clay
one_liner: GTM data platform that finds and enriches company/people records via 150+ data providers, now exposed through a public REST API, CLI, and agent plugin.
access_notes: A Clay account (including the Free plan: ~100 credits/month) provides an API key under Settings > Account > API key, usable with the public REST API and CLI. API requests/enrichments consume Actions and Data Credits, so heavy use needs a paid plan (Launch ~$185/mo, Growth ~$495/mo). The Tables query endpoint is Enterprise-only. Bringing your own data-provider keys can skip Data Credits.
api_notes: Public REST API (OpenAPI spec at developers.clay.com/openapi.json) centered on three primitives: Searches (find companies/people with structured filters, paginated), Routines (execute Clay-managed functions, custom functions, and workflows against 1-100 items, plus batch JSONL runs and result polling), and Tables (structured queries, Enterprise only), plus a user/auth endpoint. Also ships a CLI and an agent plugin for Claude Code/Codex/Cursor.
mcp_notes: Clay ships an official MCP/agent plugin (the 'Clay plugin') at https://api.clay.com/v3/mcp, letting agents search Clay data, run Functions, and create/edit/trigger Workflows. Note: unrelated third-party 'Clay' personal-CRM MCP servers also exist and should not be confused with clay.com's GTM product.
main_blocker: Meaningful API usage consumes Actions/Data Credits; the Free plan's ~100 credits/month is enough to test but real workloads require a paid plan, and Tables queries are Enterprise-gated.

evidence.claim: Clay uses API key-based authentication for all requests; provides a REST API with an OpenAPI spec, Searches/Routines/Tables endpoints, webhooks for routine completion, and an MCP plugin.
API key is found under Settings > Account > API key and is required for all Clay-native integrations.
Clay introduced a public API and CLI plus an Agent Plugin to run in Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor to search Clay data, run Functions, and create/edit/trigger Workflows.
Clay plans include a Free tier with ~100 credits/month; paid plans Launch (~$185/mo) and Growth (~$495/mo), Enterprise custom.

open_questions: Whether the Free plan actually exposes an API key or whether API key generation requires a paid (Starter/Launch) tier; exact rate limits and per-call Action costs for API requests.
